tool.name: PlasmidTrakr
tool.description: Screens assemblies against a Mash database and predicts isolate source using a trained machine learning model
tool.command:
## 1. Setup names and database #set $input_name = $assembly_input.element_identifier.replace(" ", "_").replace(".", "_") ln -s '$mash_database.fields.path' queries.msh &&
## 2. Run Mash Screen mash screen -w -i $threshold queries.msh '$assembly_input' > '${input_name}_mash.tabular' &&
## 3. Conditional Logic: Check if Mash produced hits if [ \$(wc -l < '${input_name}_mash.tabular') -ge 2 ]; then python $__tool_directory__/predict_source.py -i '${input_name}_mash.tabular' -b '$model_selection.fields.path' -t $threshold -o '$prediction_output';
## Optional: If the script ran but produced no results (e.g. filtered out) ## ensure we still provide the "No Prediction" fallback if [ ! -s '$prediction_output' ] || [ \$(wc -l < '$prediction_output') -le 1 ]; then echo -e "Run\tPredicted_Source\tConfidence_Score" > '$prediction_output'; echo -e "${input_name}\tNo Prediction\t0.0" >> '$prediction_output'; fi else ## Skip Python and create the fallback file immediately echo -e "Run\tPredicted_Source\tConfidence_Score" > '$prediction_output'; echo -e "${input_name}\tNo Prediction\t0.0" >> '$prediction_output'; fi

param.name: assembly_input
param.label: Genome Assembly / Reads
param.help: The FASTA/FASTQ file containing the isolate sequence.

param.name: mash_database
param.label: Select Mash Database
param.help: Choose the pre-computed Mash sketch database to screen against.

validator.message: No Mash databases are configured. Please contact your Galaxy administrator.

param.name: model_selection
param.label: Select Prediction Model
param.help: Choose which trained model to use for prediction.

validator.message: No prediction models are configured. Please contact your Galaxy administrator.

param.name: threshold
param.label: Mash Identity Threshold
param.help: Filter plasmid hits below this identity. Must match the threshold used for model training.

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool performs a complete workflow in a single step: it screens a genome assembly or read set against a built-in plasmid database using **mash screen**, and then feeds those plasmid hits into a pre-trained **machine learning model** to predict the original source of the isolate.
**Workflow** 1. Provide your **genome assembly (FASTA)** or raw reads. 2. Select your **Mash database** from the server's configured list. 3. Select the desired prediction model. 4. Execute to screen and predict in one step.
**Output** A tabular file containing the isolate ID, the predicted source, and a confidence score.
